Article.ArticleTitle: Effects of prenatal exercise on prenatal and postpartum depression and anxiety symptoms: A systematic review and network meta-analysis.

Abstract.AbstractText: To systematically evaluate the preventive and therapeutic effects of prenatal exercise on prenatal depression and anxiety. And the preventive effect of prenatal exercise on postpartum depression.
A systematic review and network meta-analysis.

According to the PRISMA statement, seven databases (Web of science, PubMed, Embase, Sino med, CNKI, Wanfang, VIP) were searched from inception to December 2024 to collect RCTs on the effects of prenatal exercise on the prevention or treatment of prenatal depression or anxiety and the prevention of postpartum depression or anxiety.

A total of 24 RCTs were included in this MA. The MA showed that prenatal exercise could effectively prevent prenatal depression (MD = -4.11, 95 %CI: -6.33, -1.88, P = 0.0003) and anxiety (MD = -6.76, 95 %CI: -10.29, -3.22, P = 0.0002). Furthermore, prenatal exercise can effectively treat prenatal depression (MD = -4.44, 95 %CI: -5.06, -3.82, P < 0.0001) and anxiety symptoms (MD = -3.05, 95 %CI: -5.24, -0.87, P = 0.006). Prenatal exercise could effectively prevent postpartum depression (MD = -0.95, 95 %CI: -1.87, -0.02, P = 0.04). The combination of maternal gymnastics and yoga is more effective in preventing prenatal depression and anxiety; Yoga with meditation is more effective in treating symptoms of prenatal depression; the combination of fertility dance and yoga is more effective in treating prenatal anxiety; and water-based aerobic exercise combined with resistance training is more effective in preventing postpartum depression.

Compared to the usual care, prenatal exercise can effectively prevent and treat prenatal depression and anxiety, as well as prevent postpartum depression. Future studies could consider including measures of postpartum anxiety.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Physical Education Institute, Shanxi University, Taiyuan, China.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Declaration of competing interest The authors declare that they have no competing interest relevant to the content of this review.
